references.text: [Er92e] Erd\H{o}s, P\'{a}l, Some Unsolved problems in Geometry, Number Theory and Combinatorics. Eureka (1992), 44-48.

remarks.text:
In \cite{Er92e} Erd\H{o}s asks about\[F(n)=\min_{m>n}(m-p(m)),\]and whether $n-F(n)\sim cn^{1/2}$ for some $c>0$.

See also [385].

statement.text: Is there a function $f$ with $f(n)\to \infty$ as $n\to \infty$ such that, for all large $n$, there is a composite number $m$ such that\[n+f(n)<m<n+p(m)?\](Here $p(m)$ is the least prime factor of $m$.)
